approach: Unify dual-source step tracking by removing SDD-specific fields, adding migration layer in readSpecContext, and fixing fire-and-forget race in handleStepperClick

specify.key_finding: step vs currentStep diverge in practice — specExplorerProvider prefers step, phaseCalculation uses step/substep/next/task for badges, and handleStepperClick fires updateStepProgress as fire-and-forget (no await before terminal dispatch)

plan.approach_summary: Unify dual-source step tracking by removing SDD-specific fields, adding migration layer in readSpecContext, and fixing fire-and-forget race in handleStepperClick
risks: Awaiting updateStepProgress in handleStepperClick may add minor latency before terminal command fires — mitigate by keeping write simple (<5ms)
SDD skill templates that still write old field names will work via migration layer, but should be updated to avoid confusion

T001.did: Added progress, currentTask, createdAt fields; marked step/substep/task/next/updated as @deprecated

T002.did: Added migrateContext() helper called from readSpecContext/readSpecContextSync — maps step→currentStep, substep→progress, task→currentTask at read time

T003.did: Added 7 tests for migrateContext (old→new mapping, precedence, missing fields, null handling) plus 2 integration tests for readSpecContext/readSpecContextSync

T004.did: Updated computeBadgeText signature to use currentStep/progress/currentTask, removed next param; removed contextUpdated param from computeLastUpdatedDate

T005.did: Changed all featureCtx.step→currentStep, task→currentTask, removed featureCtx.updated from computeLastUpdatedDate calls

T006.did: Replaced specContext.step ?? specContext.currentStep with just specContext.currentStep

T007.did: Made handleStepperClick await updateStepProgress before sendContentUpdateMessage — removed fire-and-forget pattern

decisions: Updated specExplorerProvider test to reflect new unified behavior — old test asserted step field preference over currentStep, which is no longer the case
Removed 'next'-based badge logic from computeBadgeText since next is no longer persisted — badges now derive from currentStep only
last_action: T007 complete — handleStepperClick now awaits updateStepProgress before terminal dispatch. All 142 tests pass.
